description_long: This object consists of four adjacent apartment buildings, each with distinct facades and architectural details. The buildings feature multiple stories, with regularly spaced windows and prominent doorways at street level. The exteriors are finished in muted tones of gray, white, and red, with some buildings displaying decorative cornices and stonework. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the central building has an ornate arched entrance. The structures are flat-fronted, typical of urban residential blocks, and appear to be constructed primarily from masonry and concrete. The overall appearance suggests a historic or early 20th-century urban architectural style.
description: A row of four multi-story apartment buildings with symmetrical windows, muted colors, and detailed facades, typical of historic urban neighborhoods.
description_view_0: A side profile showing the extreme thinness of the buildings, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_1: The front view displays all four building facades, highlighting their windows, doors, and architectural details.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile, again showing the thinness and lack of depth of the buildings.
description_view_3: A rear or alternate front view, showing the facades with slightly different window and door arrangements.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical and horizontal rows, evenly spaced across each building's facade, with some minor variations in size and grouping.
